obligation_family.name: Construction Boundary Conformance
obligation_family.meaning: A machine-readable obligation binding a disaster state to a construction rule, required evidence, current status, and explicit non-claim
obligation_family.disambiguation: CBC is the project-local obligation family; CbC refers to the general correct-by-construction engineering approach

status_promotion_rules.specified: normative_refs, required_evidence, next_action, and non_claim are present; no implementation claim follows
status_promotion_rules.implemented_partial: implementation_refs and test_refs are nonempty; uncovered required evidence and blocking dependencies remain explicit
status_promotion_rules.implemented: implementation_refs and test_refs cover the construction rule and required negative cases; blocking_dependencies is empty
status_promotion_rules.verified_scoped: implemented requirements hold and formal_refs or evidence_refs contain replayable scoped verification with assumptions and non-claims
status_promotion_rules.blocked: next_action names the blocker and blocking_dependencies identifies the unresolved prerequisite
status_promotion_rules.not_applicable: evidence_refs contains an approved rationale showing why the obligation cannot affect the selected profile

conformance_levels.ZRM-L0: typed schemas, canonical encoders, vectors, constructor rejects
conformance_levels.ZRM-L3: durable atomic machine with concurrency/crash evidence
conformance_levels.ZRM-L5: release-backed high assurance with independent review

obligations.title: Canonical resource encoding and hash binding
obligations.disaster_state: ambiguous or malleable resource bytes produce multiple identities or omit semantic fields
obligations.construction_rule: Define the versioned ResourceWireV1 binary encoding and its schema-fixed SHA-256 suite; reject trailing, noncanonical, duplicate, and unknown-critical fields; derive ResourceId from the exact inner-length-framed wire preimage
required_evidence: golden wire, suite-ID, preimage, and ResourceId vectors
obligations.next_action: retain exact draft-v1 vector compatibility, keep raw resource hashing private to the canonical codec, and preserve the reviewed syntactic-versus-semantic authority boundary in every later work package
obligations.non_claim: the frozen ResourceWireV1 and ResourceId binding feed a policy-independent intrinsic resource stage, and the supported public typed derivation path is sealed; callers can still compute or construct inert identifier bytes, and no final policy-valid ResourceV1, authenticated policy, state membership, accepted transition, or commit authority exists

obligations.title: Nonzero and domain-separated identifiers
obligations.disaster_state: zero or cross-domain identifiers collapse distinct authority domains
obligations.construction_rule: Use opaque validated newtypes; reject prohibited all-zero values; derive IDs with explicit machine/domain/schema separators
obligations.next_action: make RoleBoundIntrinsicResourceV1 obligatory for later resource-policy, logic-claim, transition, and state validation while preserving opaque construction and exact domain framing
obligations.non_claim: opaque primitives, WP3b intrinsic fields, and the WP3c body-to-role binding enforce local representation and substitution invariants only; caller-provided values remain unauthenticated and no final resource, policy, state, or transition authority follows

obligations.title: Consumed, referenced, and created roles are disjoint
obligations.disaster_state: one resource is simultaneously treated as consumed, read-only, and newly created
obligations.construction_rule: Bound all three role counts before allocation, reject within-role duplicates and all cross-role collisions in deterministic precedence, sort each valid role by ResourceId, derive role-local ordinals only from the sealed canonical partition, and bind intrinsic bodies only through their internally derived ResourceId
obligations.next_action: make RoleBoundIntrinsicResourceV1 an obligatory input to later resource-policy and logic-claim construction before proof verification, then define canonical role bytes and list roots through Class E review
obligations.non_claim: WP3a and WP3c prove only a local structural partition plus exact intrinsic-body ID, role, and ordinal consistency within a caller-proposed unauthenticated partition; they prove no complete body coverage and authenticate no policy, membership, freshness, logic, accounting, root, state, commit, or canonical ABI

obligations.title: Consumed resources exist in committed pre-state
obligations.disaster_state: a transition spends a nonexistent or stale resource
obligations.construction_rule: Verify membership against the expected pre-state root and bind the proof to resource identity and role; admit the plan only through the full-root/version CAS
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Referenced resources exist and remain unconsumed
obligations.disaster_state: a logic reads fabricated data or accidentally consumes a read-only resource
obligations.construction_rule: Verify reference membership under the expected pre-state, keep references out of nullifier/effect sets, and require the full-root/version CAS so each reference remains active at the v0.1 commit linearization point
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Nullifiers enforce exact-once consumption
obligations.disaster_state: a resource is consumed more than once
obligations.construction_rule: Derive profile-bound nullifiers, require nonmembership in the expected snapshot, and insert them atomically with effects under the state/version CAS
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Created resources are globally fresh
obligations.disaster_state: an old resource commitment is recreated after consumption or an output is duplicated
obligations.construction_rule: Reject duplicate outputs and transparent-profile historical commitment reuse in the expected snapshot, then insert outputs only under the state/version CAS
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: v0.1 historical freshness is specified only for the transparent nullifier profile; future shielded profiles require a committed equivalent

obligations.title: Resource logic covers every role and ordinal
obligations.disaster_state: a valid proof for one resource is reused to authorize another resource or role
obligations.construction_rule: Commit exactly one required LogicClaimHash per role and ordinal with no missing, duplicate, or extra claim; each verified fact binds the final transition hash, exact claim hash, verifier, policy, program, and profile
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Verified facts are sealed capabilities
obligations.disaster_state: caller-supplied metadata or proof_ok booleans create authority
obligations.construction_rule: Only verifier adapters construct private-field VerifiedFact types after full cryptographic and binding checks
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Program, key, profile, and policy are pinned
obligations.disaster_state: a valid artifact under the wrong verifier authorizes a transition
obligations.construction_rule: Verify exact program/key, proof kind, verifier parameters, policy epoch, revocation state, and the stage-appropriate statement, claim, journal draft, or accepted journal
obligations.next_action: freeze verifier-policy authority bytes and release/revocation bindings by an accepted Class E RFC, then implement the sealed WP5 registry with wrong-program, copied-content, non-production-mode, stale, revoked, and wrong-release negatives
obligations.non_claim: WP2's unauthenticated candidate-shape and admission-ID comparisons are quarantined from the default public API; this does not implement registry membership, content-derived identity, program or key identity, production-mode enforcement, release identity, revocation, trusted epoch, artifact verification, or authority facts

obligations.title: Controller authority is explicit
obligations.disaster_state: a transition uses a resource without controller approval
obligations.construction_rule: Bind signatures/capabilities to the exact transition statement, role, policy, and validity window
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Accounting rows are derived, not trusted
obligations.disaster_state: a proposer supplies balancing rows that do not reflect the actual transition
obligations.construction_rule: Derive accounting rows from validated resources and transformation outputs inside the semantic kernel
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Conserved quantities balance
obligations.disaster_state: assets or other conserved resources are created or destroyed silently
obligations.construction_rule: Enforce per-kind and per-unit debit plus authorized mint equals credit plus authorized burn using checked arithmetic
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Cross-kind transformation is explicitly authorized
obligations.disaster_state: unlike resource kinds are exchanged under an implicit or incomplete rule
obligations.construction_rule: Require a versioned transformation statement covering every nonzero delta and verify it under a governed logic
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Units cannot be mixed implicitly
obligations.disaster_state: quantities with different units are added or compared as if equivalent
obligations.construction_rule: Use opaque UnitId and quantity types; permit conversion only through an explicit transformation with rounding policy
obligations.next_action: retain exact UnitId matching in final ResourceV1 construction and permit conversion only through explicit versioned transformation rules
obligations.non_claim: the candidate enforces exact policy-unit equality only; no accounting row, conversion, rounding, transformation, or accepted transition exists

obligations.title: All arithmetic is checked and bounded
obligations.disaster_state: overflow, truncation, or lossy cast changes economic or authority semantics
obligations.construction_rule: Use explicit widths, checked operations, bounded counts, and validated conversions; prohibit floating point in consensus semantics
obligations.next_action: extend checked arithmetic and Kani evidence to complete dispatch-plan summation and WP6 accounting and transformation paths
obligations.non_claim: the evidence covers WP1 widths, exact WP3b u128 quantity and u64 epoch preservation, plus isolated WP2 policy ceilings and per-row verifier cost; no final quantity validity, complete dispatch plan, accounting arithmetic, or transformation engine exists

obligations.title: Transition statement binds all acceptance-relevant facts
obligations.disaster_state: an uncommitted field changes acceptance, ordering, replay, accounting, or authority
obligations.construction_rule: Commit proof-independent logic, transformation, authority, and DA claim descriptors with roots and counts in the transition; derive the final StatementHash without cycles; bind every verified fact to that final hash plus its exact claim hash
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Witness fields are checked, recomputed, proof-authorized, or irrelevant
obligations.disaster_state: an unconstrained witness field controls the result
obligations.construction_rule: Maintain a field-disposition table and reject any witness field lacking one of the four permitted dispositions
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Untrusted decoding is bounded and canonical
obligations.disaster_state: malformed bytes trigger excessive allocation, recursion, parser differentials, or ambiguity
obligations.construction_rule: Enforce byte, depth, count, string, and nesting caps before allocation; canonical re-encode; reject duplicate keys and unknown critical fields
obligations.next_action: authenticate machine and verifier per-artifact limits, add governed aggregate budgets before verifier dispatch, and add explicit count, depth, string, and diagnostic-JSON bounds when those wire types are introduced
obligations.non_claim: the bounded canonical decoder covers ResourceWireV1 and WP5 covers one owned byte copy under a caller-supplied per-artifact limit plus deterministic capacity-overflow and injected-refusal mapping; no authenticated policy limit, aggregate budget, real allocator-OOM evidence, generic envelope, nested collection, string, map, diagnostic JSON, proof-format canonicality, or verified-artifact decoder exists

obligations.title: Reject is a total no-op
obligations.disaster_state: a rejected transition mutates state, nullifiers, rewards, files, or external effects
obligations.construction_rule: Separate parse/authenticate/validate/plan from commit; keep plans private and commit only after final checks
required_evidence: state snapshot equality on every reject class
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Commit is atomic across effects and replay state
obligations.disaster_state: resource effects commit without nullifiers or rewards, or replay state commits without effects
obligations.construction_rule: Persist state roots, nullifiers, accepted journal, rewards, and outbox records in one atomic write set
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Stale plans fail compare-and-swap
obligations.disaster_state: two independently valid plans both commit against the same pre-state
obligations.construction_rule: Bind CommitPlan to expected state root/version and ValidationContextHash, then atomically compare the full tuple at commit
required_evidence: epoch/order-context advance race with unchanged machine state
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Concurrent execution has deterministic conflict semantics
obligations.disaster_state: race outcome changes semantic results or violates serializability
obligations.construction_rule: Serialize v0.1 global-root commits; declare read/write footprints and prove serializability before any later parallel-commit profile
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Stable rejection taxonomy and precedence
obligations.disaster_state: different implementations reveal inconsistent decisions or sensitive diagnostics
obligations.construction_rule: Define versioned reject classes, deterministic precedence, bounded public detail, and private diagnostic separation
obligations.next_action: add independent cross-language reject parity, freeze any future marker-resource reject in a new policy version, and implement the private diagnostic envelope in WP7
obligations.non_claim: stable decoder and policy-dimension precedence cover ResourceWireV1 plus the current in-memory v1 resource-kind predicate; no canonical policy reject ABI, transition-level rejection receipt, or private diagnostic schema exists

obligations.title: State and journal roots are deterministic
obligations.disaster_state: same semantic transition produces different roots across runs or languages
obligations.construction_rule: Eliminate time, RNG, locale, environment, unordered iteration, and machine paths from semantic computation
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Schema evolution is explicit and append-only or migrated
obligations.disaster_state: a field reorder or default silently changes meaning
obligations.construction_rule: Version every authority schema; reject unknown criticals; require compatibility proof or migration for breaking changes
obligations.next_action: define the old/new compatibility matrix and migration replay before any version-two wire or policy schema is accepted; keep the pre-alpha public API removals recorded in RFC-0003
obligations.non_claim: ResourceWireV1 version one is rejected or accepted exactly, and pre-alpha API quarantine has a source migration note; no version-two decoder, canonical policy codec, compatibility proof, persisted-state migration, or stable package ABI exists

obligations.title: Semantic epoch identity is proof-tree independent
obligations.disaster_state: prover grouping changes the economic identity of the same transition set
obligations.construction_rule: Define a canonical semantic transition ordering/root distinct from proof topology and prove/test grouping invariance
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Data commitment is not overclaimed as availability
obligations.disaster_state: a committed data root is reported as retrievable without policy evidence
obligations.construction_rule: Use separate VerifiedDaFact capability and profile-specific certificate verification
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Privacy claims require a complete profile and adversary model
obligations.disaster_state: computational integrity is advertised as zero knowledge or system privacy
obligations.construction_rule: Separate transparent and shielded profiles; specify leakage, network metadata, disclosure, and cryptographic assumptions
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: External and physical attestations remain scoped
obligations.disaster_state: a proof of digital processing is treated as proof of physical energy, location, identity, or truth
obligations.construction_rule: Bind external attestation source, freshness, scope, verifier, and uncertainty; preserve explicit nonclaims
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Core dependency direction remains inward
obligations.disaster_state: application, proof, database, or network concerns contaminate the semantic kernel
obligations.construction_rule: Enforce crate dependency rules and ports/adapters architecture
obligations.next_action: retain exact review of the six-crate graph and extend the allowlist only when a separately scoped core or adapter boundary is introduced
obligations.non_claim: the checker covers the current types, crypto, codec, policy, kernel, and inert verifier-input crates; it does not validate future verifier backends, adapters, stores, runtime wiring, or runtime semantic isolation

obligations.title: Unsafe code is absent or isolated with a safety case
obligations.disaster_state: undefined behavior invalidates semantic or cryptographic guarantees
obligations.construction_rule: Forbid unsafe in core; isolate adapter unsafe with safety contracts, Miri, Kani, fuzzing, and independent review
obligations.next_action: retain pinned Miri coverage and maintain an explicit unsafe inventory plus safety cases as adapters are added
obligations.non_claim: unsafe is forbidden in the current six ZRM crates; prior full-suite evidence and the targeted 12-test WP5 Miri replay cover exercised executions only, while no WP5-specific Kani, WP5 fuzz, dependency-unsafe soundness, unexercised-path, or future-adapter claim follows

obligations.title: Complexity and code quality remain locally auditable
obligations.disaster_state: critical code becomes too complex, smell-prone, pattern-incoherent, or exception-dependent for reliable review
obligations.construction_rule: Enforce complexity, length, nesting, responsibility, structural smell, antipattern, and explicit design-decision budgets with justified exceptions
obligations.next_action: retain the excellent tier with zero quality findings or exception debt, replay the behavior-and-evidence packet in hosted CI, and add a reviewed compiler-AST lane before claiming cyclomatic or cognitive-complexity coverage
obligations.non_claim: the automated reports cover five lexical complexity metrics, sixteen structural quality rules, and completeness of five required design decisions; WP5 reuses ZRM-DESIGN-003 without adding a pattern, and the reports do not expand macros, classify semantic criticality, compute cyclomatic or cognitive complexity, detect every diff-aware smell, or prove universal design-pattern optimality

obligations.title: Critical guards are mutation-tested
obligations.disaster_state: tests pass even after a critical guard is removed or inverted
obligations.construction_rule: Maintain hand-authored mutants for every critical invariant and run general mutation testing
obligations.next_action: retain zero missed generated mutants and named zero-quantity, API-quarantine, protocol-ceiling, selected-limit, and allocation-refusal mutants as resource, transition, accounting, state, registry, and commit guards are added
obligations.non_claim: recorded campaigns cover WP1, pre-RFC WP2 internal arithmetic and policy dimensions, the authority-API quarantine, WP3a, WP3b, WP3c, and the bounded WP5 artifact boundary; compile-fail/API checks do not prove a semantic registry, and no complete transition kernel, authority registry, verifier dispatch, state, persistence, or commit guard exists

obligations.title: Parsers and proof envelopes are fuzzed
obligations.disaster_state: untrusted input reaches panic, UB, hang, or resource exhaustion
obligations.construction_rule: Maintain coverage-guided, structure-aware fuzz targets and commit minimized regressions
obligations.next_action: accumulate sustained fuzz duration, retain the resource-dimension full-width grammar, add canonical policy decoders only after the Class E ABI RFC, commit minimized failures, and add later proof-envelope grammars
obligations.non_claim: recorded campaigns cover the ResourceWireV1 decoder, quarantined WP2 internal arithmetic, the policy-dimension predicate, WP3b intrinsic construction, and patch-bound WP3c placement and stale-body substitution; they grant no SHA-256 proof, canonical policy, authenticated cost model, proof envelope, registry, final resource, or complete semantic-kernel assurance

obligations.title: Reference and optimized semantics agree
obligations.disaster_state: an optimization changes decisions, rows, roots, or journals
obligations.construction_rule: Maintain a simple reference interpreter and differential tests against every optimized path
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Formal models are linked to runtime semantics
obligations.disaster_state: a theorem proves a model that does not match the implementation
obligations.construction_rule: Use shared constants, vectors, extraction, verified implementation, or a reviewed refinement mapping
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Proof-task rewards are exact-once
obligations.disaster_state: duplicate, reassigned, late, or challenged proofs create multiple rewards
obligations.construction_rule: Model task, assignment, completion, challenge, timeout, escrow, and reward as consumed/created resources
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Generated and agent-authored code is treated as untrusted
obligations.disaster_state: AI-generated code bypasses design, review, testing, or provenance requirements
obligations.construction_rule: Require task/invariant plan, minimal diff, evidence log, human review, and all ordinary gates; forbid self-promotion
obligations.next_action: retain the completed RFC-0003 maintainer review, obtain final human behavior and assurance-evidence review for the remaining candidate slices after clean hosted gates, retain isolated oracle and adversarial artifacts, and prove live distinct-reviewer branch controls before authority or release promotion
obligations.non_claim: WP1 has scoped human approval and RFC-0003 has explicit maintainer semantic approval; these approvals do not promote the other candidate slices or replace an external production audit, and static CODEOWNERS do not prove live host enforcement

obligations.title: Dependencies and build tooling are governed
obligations.disaster_state: a vulnerable or malicious dependency/build script compromises the TCB
obligations.construction_rule: Minimize dependencies; review features/build scripts/proc macros; run audit/deny/vet and lock critical versions
obligations.next_action: validate the hosted deny/BOM gate, adopt cargo-vet only after maintainers approve the critical trust criteria or reviewed audit imports, and add standardized plus compiled-artifact BOM exports during WP13
obligations.non_claim: the ZRM-native reports inventory locked Cargo source components, dependency edges, executable build targets, and registered cryptography; they do not establish cargo-vet trust, SPDX or CycloneDX conformance, compiled-artifact contents, signed provenance, reproducibility, or a release-complete supply chain

obligations.title: Release artifacts carry verifiable provenance
obligations.disaster_state: artifacts cannot be traced to reviewed source and builder policy
obligations.construction_rule: Produce SLSA provenance, verification summary, SBOM/CBOM, source/toolchain hashes, test/formal reports, signatures, and nonclaims
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Reproducibility claims require independent bit-identical builds
obligations.disaster_state: same-host or path-dependent builds are called reproducible
obligations.construction_rule: Use isolated independent clean builds and compare specified artifacts bit-for-bit; record environment and path policy
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: Claims never exceed evidence
obligations.disaster_state: docs or UI call a local test production-ready, private, available, or formally verified
obligations.construction_rule: Gate reserved claim language through a machine-readable claims/conformance matrix and human review
obligations.next_action: implement the rule and attach replayable evidence before status promotion
obligations.non_claim: specification text alone does not satisfy this obligation

obligations.title: No secret or sensitive diagnostic leakage
obligations.disaster_state: errors, Debug, logs, or evidence bundles disclose witnesses, keys, or private state
obligations.construction_rule: Separate public reject receipts from private diagnostics; redact/zeroize; bound outputs; disable secret Debug
obligations.next_action: extend constant redaction and disclosure review to future proof artifacts, witnesses, private state, log sinks, errors, and evidence bundles; add zeroization where private profiles introduce secrets
obligations.non_claim: default opaque values and ResourceWireV1 32-byte array candidates are redacted, nested intrinsic formatting is nonce-value-independent, and WP5 prevents ordinary Debug formatting of BoundedArtifactV1; numeric wire scalars remain visible, and no logging framework, private witness type, zeroization policy, log-sink audit, or complete private diagnostic envelope exists

obligations.title: Resource exhaustion is bounded end to end
obligations.disaster_state: validly shaped input causes excessive CPU, memory, disk, proof, or verifier work
obligations.construction_rule: Commit explicit per-profile byte/count/depth/storage limits plus one governed deterministic verifier-cost model; precompute and bound the complete verifier dispatch charge before expensive work
required_evidence: cost-row and complete-dispatch-plan vectors with checked-overflow cases
backend evidence that governed worst-case work remains within each cost row
obligations.next_action: authenticate machine and verifier artifact limits, add governed aggregate artifact budgets, freeze cost-model authority bytes, implement sorted authenticated cost rows and the complete pre-dispatch cost plan, then add backend worst-case and end-to-end load evidence in WP5 and WP9
obligations.non_claim: WP1 byte bounds, bounded WP3b field validation, bounded WP3c role lookup, WP2 machine ceilings and internal candidate arithmetic, plus WP5's caller-supplied single-artifact ceiling and deterministic refusal mapping are implemented; the default public API cannot produce a caller-row quote, and no real allocator-OOM, aggregate-budget, authenticated cost model, governed charge, complete dispatch plan, backend work bound, storage runtime, rate limit, or end-to-end DoS claim exists

obligations.title: Policy-bound resource dimensions are positive, bounded, and mode-canonical
obligations.disaster_state: a zero, over-maximum, wrong-unit, or mode-invalid quantity enters a policy-validated resource, or noncanonical lifecycle policy shapes create divergent accepted state
obligations.construction_rule: Resolve one active resource-kind policy, require exact UnitId equality and a positive quantity no greater than its maximum, enforce mode-specific quantity shape, and reject noncanonical lifecycle policy maxima with deterministic constructor and resource precedence
required_evidence: all-mode zero, maximum, unit, and lifecycle decision tables
frozen prior-specification oracle plus proposed semantic-delta oracle
full-width checked predicate proof and resource-dimension fuzzing
human approval of the lifecycle maximum amendment
obligations.next_action: apply the accepted predicate only after authenticated active-policy resolution in final ResourceV1 construction, retain the RFC-0003 error precedence, and define any marker permission in a future version
obligations.non_claim: RFC-0003 approval fixes the pre-authority predicate but grants no runtime authority; the implemented predicate still consumes an inert policy candidate and candidate dimensions only, with no canonical policy bytes, active-policy authentication, final ResourceV1 capability, accounting proof, marker permission, accepted transition, or commit

obligations.title: Scoped four-column reference carrier preserves balance, support, and capacity
obligations.disaster_state: a signed net or resource-only projection erases gross flow or authority support, or an aggregate quantity exceeds the proved carrier capacity
obligations.construction_rule: Within the non-normative natural-number reference carrier, derive consumed, created, authorized-burn, and authorized-mint totals per exact dimension and prove balance, zero-support reflection, resource-only noninjectivity, and U32-by-U128 capacity below U256
required_evidence: executable four-column reference model and negative projection cases
Lean balance, support, noninjectivity, and capacity theorems
obligations.next_action: define the canonical finite-map and accepted-leaf refinement, then prove the selected Rust U256 and circuit representations implement this scoped carrier without field wrap or dropped support
obligations.non_claim: The verified scope is pure natural-number and reference-model arithmetic under U32 leaf-count and U128 per-leaf premises; it establishes no canonical bytes, authenticated opening, Rust or circuit refinement, governed profile, recursive proof, RFC approval, or authority

obligations.title: Scoped numeric aggregation result and definedness are proof-tree independent
obligations.disaster_state: different binary groupings of the same bounded numeric leaves change whether aggregation succeeds or change its four-column result
obligations.construction_rule: Within the non-normative numeric carrier, require componentwise monotone merge and prove every nonempty binary tree succeeds exactly when the complete totals fit and then returns the same result
required_evidence: exhaustive small-tree grouping comparison and deterministic bounded exploration
Lean arbitrary-tree definedness and total-overflow-carrier theorems
counterexample retaining the rejected bounded-signed carrier
obligations.next_action: link the scoped numeric theorem to canonical sparse maps, exact ordered provenance, the governed profile, Rust and guest implementations, and existing CBC-027 semantic-root identity
obligations.non_claim: The verified scope is the numeric fold and checked definedness model only; it does not prove canonical map ordering or bytes, retained-opening binding, ordered cryptographic coverage, SemanticSegmentHash equality, Rust or circuit refinement, or full protocol proof-tree independence

obligations.title: RFC-0004 aggregate, profile, and coverage encodings are exact and domain separated
obligations.disaster_state: ambiguous, cross-domain, cross-profile, reordered, duplicate, truncated, or trailing bytes identify an accounting aggregate or provenance object
obligations.construction_rule: Use manual versioned big-endian encoders and schema-fixed domains for profile, aggregate row, row list, coverage entry, coverage manifest, and retained opening; reject noncanonical forms and require independent complete vectors before approval
required_evidence: profile bytes and ID plus canonical empty-root vectors
row, row-list, coverage-entry, coverage-manifest, and retained-opening vectors
obligations.next_action: add profile and empty-root vectors, freeze an existing AccountingRowV1 opening and retained wrapper, complete all-field and cross-domain mutants, and obtain independent cross-language agreement before any ABI claim
obligations.non_claim: Current proposed vectors cover aggregate row and row-list hashing, coverage entry and manifest hashing, selected capacity cases, order and multiplicity mutants, and U32 framing only; profile bytes and ID, empty roots, retained opening bytes, frozen leaf-row bytes, every-field mutation, cross-language ABI agreement, and approval remain absent

obligations.title: Retained openings bind exact authenticated accepted-journal accounting rows
obligations.disaster_state: a prover supplies fabricated, unavailable, truncated, reordered, or mismatched leaf rows while claiming they came from an accepted journal
obligations.construction_rule: Before leaf projection, bound and strictly decode the retained opening, authenticate the exact AcceptedJournal hash, row bytes, row count, row hashes, and AccountingRowsRoot, and fail closed when any opening is unavailable or inconsistent
required_evidence: frozen AccountingRowV1 opening and root/count vectors
malicious count, length, truncation, order, duplicate, and allocation-refusal fuzzing
obligations.next_action: freeze the accepted-journal accounting-row ABI, implement a bounded retained-opening parser and authenticated journal binding, define governed retention, and add negative replay and fuzz evidence
obligations.non_claim: No frozen AccountingRowV1 canonical opening, authenticated AcceptedJournal adapter, root and count replay, retained-byte parser, proving-time availability guarantee, retention policy, or data-availability result exists

obligations.title: Ordered coverage preserves every authenticated leaf and dimension occurrence
obligations.disaster_state: aggregation omits, duplicates, permutes, relabels, or collapses a leaf accounting occurrence, authority root, transformation root, row-hash role, or dimension
obligations.construction_rule: Derive one typed coverage entry for every opened leaf-row occurrence, preserve multiplicity in exact leaf-position and dimension order, concatenate manifests exactly at parents, and keep aggregate totals incapable of granting authority
required_evidence: missing, extra, permuted, duplicate, relabeled, and wrong-digest-role negatives
obligations.next_action: bind coverage derivation to the retained accepted-journal opening, replace abstract provenance fixtures with frozen leaf bytes, and prove exact ordered cryptographic refinement and availability behavior
obligations.non_claim: The model uses abstract provenance identifiers, the candidate vector uses an opaque existing-leaf AccountingRowHash fixture, and Lean CoversExactly is a multiset relation for numeric aggregation; none proves accepted-journal binding, actual leaf bytes, ordered cryptographic coverage refinement, availability, or authority

obligations.title: Governed accounting profile and semantic summary reject substitution and mixed versions
obligations.disaster_state: an aggregate is replayed under a different profile, limit set, journal schema, semantic context, proof program, or predecessor interpretation
obligations.construction_rule: Select one governed AccountingAggregationProfileId and bind its exact roots, counts, retained-byte total, accepted-journal schema, semantic context, and compatibility rules into the versioned semantic summary and proof statement
required_evidence: approved profile bytes, identity, and exact deployable limits
profile, schema, context, program, key, and release substitution negatives
migration, historical replay, rollback, and predecessor matrix
obligations.next_action: obtain RFC approval and exact limits, freeze the semantic-summary schema and profile ID, identify any predecessor use, then implement policy selection plus migration and substitution tests
obligations.non_claim: No approved limits, canonical profile-ID vector, governed profile selector, SemanticSegmentHash schema, proof-statement binding, migration or replay matrix, predecessor audit, or cross-profile substitution test exists

obligations.title: Accounting quantity, count, byte, and work bounds fail closed across trees
obligations.disaster_state: crafted dimensions, counts, retained bytes, carries, field limbs, or merge trees cause overflow, allocation abuse, proof-field wrap, excessive work, or inconsistent reject precedence
obligations.construction_rule: Apply governed quantity, dimension, coverage, retained-byte, leaf, frame, and verifier-cost limits before expensive work; use checked monotone arithmetic and stable reject precedence at leaves and parents
required_evidence: executed ESSO mutant campaign for overflow, row dropping, swaps, bounds, and coverage omission
Rust U256, carry, range, Kani, property, differential, and fuzz evidence
worst-case memory, cycle, proof-size, and storage benchmarks
obligations.next_action: execute and record the ESSO plan, choose deployable limits from benchmarks, implement bounded retained parsing and Rust U256 arithmetic, constrain circuit limbs and carries, and add deterministic reject and resource evidence
obligations.non_claim: Scoped evidence covers natural-number quantity capacity, selected model count caps, and U32 digest-list framing; the ESSO campaign is explicitly unexecuted, and no retained-byte parser, allocation refusal, deployable limits, runtime reject implementation, benchmark, Rust U256 refinement, circuit constraint, or proof-cost bound exists

obligations.title: Only an exactly bound recursive verifier constructs the sealed accounting fact
obligations.disaster_state: host-proposed totals, roots, booleans, proofs, or callback results create admission, state, commit, mint, burn, transformation, or aggregation authority
obligations.construction_rule: Dispatch through a governed sealed recursive backend, verify exact program, key, release, profile, semantic summary, children, accepted journals, and authenticated output, then construct a sealed postcommit aggregation fact that cannot authorize precommit admission or commit
required_evidence: sealed fact forgery and phase-substitution tests
wrong program, key, release, profile, summary, child, journal, and output negatives
recursive guest and reference differential tests
obligations.next_action: freeze the governed statement and verified-fact API, implement the recursive verifier adapter and guest, bind every exact identity and output, and independently review the postcommit-only authority boundary
obligations.non_claim: No sealed accounting aggregation fact, verifier adapter, exact program or key identity, recursive guest, circuit proof, admission-separation test, accepted-journal binding, commit integration, or independent proof-system review exists

obligations.title: Net projection is a governed boundary result and never a recursive carrier or no-op claim
obligations.disaster_state: a signed net re-enters recursive aggregation, an untrusted Boolean claims balance, or zero net is treated as semantic no-op or authority
obligations.construction_rule: Compute net only after successful four-column aggregation by canonical compare-and-subtract for a governed selected dimension set, return a typed sealed boundary fact, and prohibit its use as recursive state, admission authority, or no-op evidence
required_evidence: selected-dimension-set root and governed predicate tests
net-as-carrier, host-Boolean, authority, and no-op rejection tests
obligations.next_action: freeze the signed-magnitude and selected-set encodings, define the governed boundary predicate and sealed fact, then add runtime, guest, authority-substitution, and semantic-no-op negatives
obligations.non_claim: Only mathematical net projection and no-op separation are modeled; no canonical signed-magnitude codec, selected-dimension-set root, governed typed predicate, sealed boundary fact, runtime or guest binding, or authority exists

obligations.title: Transient V2 placement and authority encodings are exact and domain separated
obligations.disaster_state: a transient resource is confused with consumed, referenced, or created placement, or V2 bytes are decoded under V1 authority meanings
obligations.construction_rule: Keep every V1 byte and ResourceId meaning unchanged; define a versioned fourth placement with pairwise-disjoint C, R, O, and Q sets, strict bounded decoders, new authority domains, canonical re-encoding equality, and independent complete vectors
required_evidence: frozen V1 non-regression corpus and V2 placement byte tables
two independent complete V2 vector replays including TransitionIdV2 cross-version separation
unknown tag, duplicate placement, overlap, truncation, trailing-byte, and cross-domain mutants
migration and historical V1 replay evidence
obligations.next_action: approve or revise RFC-0005, freeze the exact V2 schemas, domains, and transition-ID derivation, then add independent codecs, golden vectors, cross-version replay, and V1 byte-preservation tests
obligations.non_claim: No V2 codec, frozen domain, TransitionIdV2 derivation, golden vector, independent replay, migration implementation, or approved transient ABI exists

obligations.title: Every transient has exact birth, death, producer, and consumer authority
obligations.disaster_state: a transient is created or retired without its own resource logic, is covered by an undifferentiated fact, or is produced or consumed zero, multiple, or mismatched times
obligations.construction_rule: Bind each Q resource to exactly one TransientBirth logic fact, one TransientDeath logic fact, one producer output use, and one consumer input use with exact role, ordinal, resource ID, claim hash, policy root, statement hash, and sealed verifier identities
required_evidence: missing, duplicate, swapped, wrong-role, wrong-ordinal, and wrong-resource negative tests
wrong program, claim, statement, policy, output, and verifier substitution tests
sealed-fact forgery and consumer no-op tests
reference, optimized, and proof-adapter differential corpus
obligations.next_action: freeze V2 logic and transformation-use claims, implement sealed exact-binding fact constructors, and add the complete dual-authorization mutation atlas
obligations.non_claim: No V2 logic claim, transformation-use claim, sealed transient fact, verifier adapter, proof guest, differential corpus, or authority exists

obligations.title: Transient freshness and planned nullifiers prevent replay admission
obligations.disaster_state: a transient already exists, has been retired before, is checked at a stale epoch, is inserted into active state, or is omitted from the planned nullifier effects
obligations.construction_rule: Require every Q resource to be absent from active state and historical nullifiers at one authenticated epoch, never plan Q for active insertion, derive exactly one planned nullifier effect for every Q resource, and bind the complete freshness reads and planned writes to the private validated plan
required_evidence: active-presence, prior-nullifier, stale-epoch, duplicate-Q, and omitted-plan tests
exact authenticated freshness-read and planned-nullifier construction tests
reject-is-no-op tests for every precommit freshness failure
bounded reference-state and private-plan refinement evidence
obligations.next_action: freeze the authenticated freshness statement and planned state effects, then implement the reference-state oracle and private Q-nullifier plan with exact negative and refinement tests
obligations.non_claim: No transient freshness oracle, authenticated state-plan implementation, planned-effect refinement, or replay-admission proof exists; durable insertion, concurrency, and crash recovery remain CBC-061 obligations

obligations.title: Transient dependency graphs are bounded, acyclic, unique, and boundary anchored
obligations.disaster_state: a transient identity has multiple producers or consumers, or the graph contains a cycle, an all-transient component, nondeterministic traversal, or resource-exhausting work
obligations.construction_rule: Derive one deterministic producer-to-consumer edge per Q resource, require unique endpoints for each identity, permit bounded branch and merge through distinct identities, reject cycles and same-identity multiple consumers, require each connected Q component to have a persistent input and persistent created output, and enforce governed claim, per-claim use, aggregate use, edge, depth, byte, memory, and verifier-work limits before allocation or expensive checks
required_evidence: cycle, same-identity multiple-consumer, duplicate endpoint, disconnected component, missing persistent anchor, and ordering mutants
deterministic topological and boundary-anchoring property tests
per-claim and aggregate transformation-use allocation refusal plus worst-case memory, cycle, proof-size, and journal benchmarks
bounded graph theorem and runtime refinement evidence
obligations.next_action: collect two real workflow traces, select exact protocol and strict-default limits from benchmarks, and prove the bounded deterministic graph oracle before implementation
obligations.non_claim: No approved workflow need, numeric limit, graph implementation, benchmark, property corpus, bounded theorem, or runtime refinement exists

obligations.title: Transient quantities contribute to both gross accounting sides without granting authority
obligations.disaster_state: transient quantity is omitted, double counted, netted away before validation, assigned inconsistent dimensions, or treated as mint, burn, transformation, or no-op authority
obligations.construction_rule: Project every Q quantity exactly once into the producer claim's created gross side and exactly once into the consumer claim's consumed gross side for its exact kind and unit, require every per-claim delta row to equal its exact use-derived projection plus separately authorized mint or burn rows, require the checked composition to equal global accounting, preserve RFC-0004 multiplicity and coverage, and prohibit cancellation or zero net from authorizing any lifecycle
required_evidence: missing-side, double-count, wrong-dimension, overflow, order, and multiplicity mutants
unauthorized birth, death, mint, burn, transformation, and net-as-authority negatives
per-claim use-to-delta, global composition, and RFC-0004 aggregate differential vectors
gross-flow preservation and runtime refinement theorem
obligations.next_action: freeze the V2 accounting projection, add reference and RFC-0004 vectors, then prove and implement exact gross-side multiplicity without changing AccountingRowV1
obligations.non_claim: No transient accounting projection, runtime check, vector, aggregate integration, arithmetic proof, or authorization evidence exists; equal gross totals do not authorize or prove a valid transient

obligations.title: Transient state, nullifier, semantic-effect, and journal changes commit atomically
obligations.disaster_state: a rejected or crashed transition leaves a transient active, inserts only some nullifiers, publishes a mismatched journal, or reports acceptance before a linearizable durable commit
obligations.construction_rule: Privately plan active-state deletion and creation plus C and Q nullifier insertion in the pre-journal semantic-effect commitment; derive the successor accepted journal without a hash cycle; then atomically commit semantic effects, journal, replay, state-head, admission, and audit metadata or none, bind the final journal bytes, and preserve idempotent recovery without exposing a forgeable plan or capability
required_evidence: reject-is-no-op tests at each validation and commit failure point
same-Q concurrent histories and crash matrix before, during, and after durable commit plus idempotent retry
semantic-effect, journal, replay, state-head, admission, and audit hash-dependency acyclicity check
wrong journal, root, count, semantic-effect, nullifier, and receipt binding negatives
linearizability model and persistent-adapter differential histories
obligations.next_action: coordinate the successor journal and semantic-effect schema with RFC-0001, then model and implement private plans, atomic commit, crash recovery, and accepted-journal binding
obligations.non_claim: No V2 journal, semantic-effect schema, private commit plan, durable adapter, crash matrix, linearizability proof, or accepted transient journal exists

obligations.title: V2 activation, replay, recursion, and privacy claims remain version scoped
obligations.disaster_state: a V2 transient transition is interpreted under V1, replayed across machine or policy identity, mixed into unsupported recursion, rolled back unsafely, strands a live persistent output, preserves a pre-rollback plan, or is represented to users as private or ARM-equivalent
obligations.construction_rule: Govern explicit V2 activation and feature rollback, bind machine, domain, policy, schema, program, release, and profile identities, reject unsupported mixed-version recursion, replay historical V1 bytes unchanged, advance policy root and state version on rollback, retain a Q-empty compatibility path for live V2-policy persistent outputs, stale every old plan and fact, and expose that the transparent profile reveals transient identifiers, counts, nullifiers, and timing and is stricter than membership-optional ARM ephemerality
required_evidence: activation, downgrade, rollback, historical replay, and mixed V1/V2 recursion matrix including live-output consumability and stale pre-rollback plans
cross-machine, domain, policy, schema, program, release, and profile substitution tests
public API and BDD review for accurate transient and privacy language
two independent Class E reviews including an authority-boundary reviewer
obligations.next_action: obtain semantic approval, freeze activation, non-stranding rollback, and migration rules, define the first recursive profile, add substitution, stale-plan, live-output, and historical replay tests, and complete independent authority and UX claim reviews
obligations.non_claim: No approved profile, activation mechanism, non-stranding rollback or migration implementation, mixed-version recursive proof, privacy property, unlinkability, ARM-equivalent behavior, or independent Class E approval exists
